title: Defining Popup Forms
text: Defining Popup Forms Use the Window Type and other component instance properties to define a popup form. For more information, see Window Type (WindowType) and Popup Window . To define a popup form, set the Window Type property to Popup . You can do this in the Window Properties of the form, or in ProcScript when creating the component instance. For example, a form with the following code defines a popup form in the default position. (The Background Color of the popup form is Info .) trigger detail; of FLD1 newinstance "POPUP_FRM", "popup", "windowtype=popup" activate "popup" end Copy By default, the popup form is positioned below the FLD1 field, starting from the bottom left corner. To change the position of the popup form, set the Position window property. This can only be done in ProcScript. For example, the following code changes the anchor point for the popup window to the bottom right corner, and displays to below and to the left of that corner: trigger detail; of FLD1 newinstance "POPUP_FRM", "popup", "windowtype=popup ; position=BOTTOMRIGHT_DOWNLEFT" activate "popup" end Copy Optionally, set other properties of the popup window using the Properties Inspector or the newinstance ProcScript statement. In the Properties Inspector, click the More icon ( ) of the component's Window Properties to open the window properties dialog. In ProcScript when creating the component instance. In this example, POPUP_NORMAL is a normal form with the Caption and Close properties set to true . When displayed as a popup form, these properties are ignored by default. However, they are applied when InheritWinProps is true . trigger detail; of FLD1 newinstance "POPUP_NORMAL", "popup", "windowtype=popup ; InheritWinProps=true ; position=BOTTOMRIGHT_UPRIGHT" activate "popup" end Copy For more information, see InheritWinProps and Auto Close (AutoClose) . To refine the position of the popup form, so that it can be displayed from a specific point inside or outside the widget. set the PopupRect widget property. This can only be done in ProcScript using the $fieldproperties function. For example, the following code defines a rectangle of 100 by 10 pixels, starting 4 characters to the right and 1 character down from the upper left corner of the field. trigger detail; of FLD1 newinstance "POPUP_FRM", "popup", "windowtype=popup" $fieldproperties(FLD1) = "popuprect=4,1,100px,10px" activate "popup" end Copy Related Links Popup Forms Popup forms are displayed as secondary windows that are anchored to a field, although their position is completely configurable. They can be used in situations where you want to display or gather a limited amount of data, for example, in a ribbon bar or super tooltip. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
